rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the ground-truth reviewer's actual concern (hasattr checks not verifying reduce_scatter behaves correctly), so recall is near-zero for both. X mostly confirms the PR is correct and ends in APPROVE with a single vague, low-actionability nit; its claims are accurate but thin. Y digs deeper with five concrete, file/line-anchored findings (untested early-return branch, unconditional collective call on non-MoE paths, missing guard/docstrings), each backed by cited evidence, making it far more actionable even though a couple are minor nits.
